paper_title: Study of Pollinator Visitation in Oenothera fruticosa

methods.description: This method involves observing and recording pollinator visitation to Oenothera fruticosa flowers to determine visitation rates and identify pollinator types. This is crucial for understanding the pollination ecology of the species.

steps.description: Tag 122 O. fruticosa plants distributed across a 12-ha grassland before flowering.
steps.searchable_summary: tag Oenothera fruticosa plants for study

steps.description: Randomly select one flower per plant to follow from date of anthesis through to fruit set.
Record diurnal insect visitation on each flower for approximately 1 h using high-definition video cameras during pollinator activity (08:00-16:00 hours) on warm, sunny days.
steps.searchable_summary: record insect visitation using video cameras

steps.description: Identify pollinators from the video footage and determine pollinator visitation rates, considering only insects that contact the reproductive structures of the flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze video footage to identify pollinators and calculate visitation rates

steps.description: Quantify pollinator visitation rates of four functional groups: large bees, small bees, large flies, and small flies.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify visitation rates by pollinator group
